---
title: Package Selection
description: Choose the right Elata package and avoid internal-only workflows.
---

Use this page when you already know you are integrating into an existing app
and want package-level guidance.

If you are starting a new app, do not start here. Use [Quickstart](/quickstart)
or [Build A Browser App](/browser-apps) first.

## Recommended Default

If you are starting fresh, use `@elata-biosciences/create-elata-demo`.

That is the shortest path to a running app and the best comparison baseline.

For open source apps that combine rPPG, EEG, and Web Bluetooth, see
[Example applications](/example-apps).

## Choose By Outcome

| You want to... | Use this | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Scaffold a new demo app | `@elata-biosciences/create-elata-demo` | recommended first step |
| Run camera-based rPPG in a browser app | `@elata-biosciences/rppg-web` | primary integration; start with `createRppgSession()` |
| Run EEG WASM APIs in the browser | `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web` | optional; signal processing, models, and WASM helpers |
| Enable a Muse-compatible headset over Bluetooth | `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web-ble` | add **with** `eeg-web` |

## Camera-Based rPPG (Primary)

Use `@elata-biosciences/rppg-web` when you need:

- browser-side rPPG processing
- packaged WASM backend loading
- demo helpers for camera-driven prototypes

In app terms, this is the package you use when the camera itself is the sensor
and your app wants pulse-style metrics without extra hardware for things like
stress-aware UX, deception mechanics, or biofeedback-oriented health flows.

## Browser EEG (Optional)

Use `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web` when you need:

- browser-side EEG WASM APIs
- signal processing and model functions
- shared types used by higher-level browser integrations

In app terms, this is the package you use when your product logic needs EEG
features or analyses in the browser for things like meditation state, focus
feedback, neurofeedback, or performance-style training loops.

## Web Bluetooth: Enable A Muse-Compatible Headset

Use `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web-ble` when you need:

- browser BLE discovery and streaming
- normalized headband frames
- a transport layer that plugs into the Elata EEG web stack

Install `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web` alongside it. This package **turns on
Bluetooth** for supported headsets; it does not replace the EEG WASM APIs in
`eeg-web`.

## What This Page Is Not

- It is not the primary chooser page. That is [Build A Browser App](/browser-apps).
- It is not the fastest new-app path. That is [Quickstart](/quickstart).
- It is not the full install walkthrough. Use the existing-app tutorials for that.

## Internal Paths To Avoid Unless You Mean It

- Do not start new apps with `./run.sh sync-to`.
- Do not treat in-repo demos as the primary consumer onboarding path.
- Do not assume a scaffolded app is broken until you check whether `pnpm`
  attached to a parent workspace.

## Related Docs

- Main chooser page: [Build A Browser App](/browser-apps)
- Example applications: [Example applications](/example-apps)
- Fast new-app path: [Quickstart](/quickstart)
- Existing-app rPPG tutorial: [Add Camera-Based rPPG To An Existing Browser App](/tutorials/rppg-existing-app)
- Existing-app EEG tutorial: [Add EEG To An Existing Browser App](/tutorials/eeg-existing-app)
- Existing-app BLE tutorial: [Stream Muse-Compatible EEG Over Web Bluetooth](/tutorials/eeg-ble-live-stream)

## When The Alternative Path Is Correct

Only use `./run.sh sync-to` when you are actively modifying
`packages/eeg-web` inside this monorepo and want to install the local package
into another app for iteration.
